arguments.file_text:
# Help Chatbot — Implementation Plan

## Problem
Build a web-based, LLM-driven chatbot using Blazor that answers customer questions by searching internal product PDF documentation. Use Postgres (with pgvector) for data storage and a local `Docs/` folder for PDF files.

## Approach — RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation)
This is a **RAG pipeline**: ingest PDFs → chunk text → generate embeddings → store in pgvector → on each user query, retrieve relevant chunks → inject into LLM prompt → return grounded answer with source attribution.

Per the .NET AI technology-selection guidance:
- **Microsoft.Extensions.AI** (`IChatClient` / `IEmbeddingGenerator`) — LLM abstraction layer (single prompt→response, no agentic loops needed)
- **OpenAI** — concrete LLM provider (gpt-4o-mini for chat, text-embedding-3-small for embeddings)
- **Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions** + **pgvector** — vector similarity search
- **UglyToad.PdfPig** — PDF text extraction (MIT licensed)
- **Npgsql / EF Core** — Postgres access for chat history
- **Blazor Server** (interactive SSR) — real-time chat UI with streaming responses

## Architecture

```
┌──────────────┐       ┌──────────────────┐       ┌────────────┐
│  Blazor UI   │──────▶│  ChatService     │──────▶│  OpenAI    │
│  (Chat page) │◀──────│  (RAG pipeline)  │◀──────│  LLM API   │
└──────────────┘       └────────┬─────────┘       └────────────┘
                                │
                    ┌───────────┼───────────┐
                    ▼                       ▼
            ┌──────────────┐       ┌──────────────┐
            │  pgvector    │       │  Postgres    │
            │  (embeddings)│       │  (chat hist) │
            └──────────────┘       └──────────────┘
                    ▲
                    │
            ┌──────────────┐       ┌──────────┐
            │ Ingestion    │◀──────│ Docs/    │
            │ Service      │       │ (PDFs)   │
            └──────────────┘       └──────────┘
```

## Todos

### 1. Project setup & packages
- Add NuGet packages: `Microsoft.Extensions.AI`, `OpenAI`, `Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions`, pgvector MEVD connector, `UglyToad.PdfPig`, `Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL`, Blazor component packages
- Configure `appsettings.json` with Postgres connection string and AI options (model IDs, temperature)
- Set up user-secrets for API key storage (never in source)

### 2. Data models
- `DocumentChunk` — record for vector store (Id, Text, Embedding, SourceFile, PageNumber)
- `ChatMessage` — EF entity for chat history (Id, SessionId, Role, Content, Timestamp)
- `ChatSession` — EF entity (Id, CreatedAt)

### 3. Postgres + pgvector setup
- EF Core `ChatDbContext` for chat history tables
- MEVD vector store registration for `DocumentChunk`
- Startup migration / ensure-created logic

### 4. PDF ingestion service
- `IngestionService` — scans `Docs/` folder for PDFs
- Extract text with PdfPig, split into semantic chunks (~500 tokens, paragraph-boundary aware)
- Generate embeddings via `IEmbeddingGenerator<string, Embedding<float>>`
- Upsert chunks into pgvector store
- Runs at startup (skip already-ingested files) and can be triggered manually

### 5. RAG chat service
- `ChatService.AskAsync(string question, string sessionId)`
- Embed the user query
- Search pgvector for top-5 relevant chunks (min similarity 0.75)
- Build system prompt with retrieved context + source attribution instructions
- Call `IChatClient` with streaming (`GetStreamingResponseAsync`)
- Persist Q&A to chat history in Postgres

### 6. Blazor UI
- `Components/Pages/Chat.razor` — chat interface with message bubbles, input box, send button
- Streaming token display via `StreamRendering`
- Chat history sidebar showing past sessions
- Source attribution displayed beneath answers (file name + page number)
- `Components/Layout/` — minimal layout with app title

### 7. Service registration (Program.cs)
- Register `IChatClient` via `AddChatClient` with OpenAI provider + retry middleware
- Register `IEmbeddingGenerator` for embeddings
- Register MEVD vector store with pgvector
- Register EF Core with Npgsql
- Register `IngestionService`, `ChatService`
- Set temperature=0, pin model versions, set max output tokens
- Add health checks for OpenAI and Postgres

### 8. Configuration & security
- `appsettings.json` — non-secret config (model IDs, chunk size, connection string template)
- User-secrets for `AI:ApiKey`
- `IOptions<AiOptions>` pattern for all AI config

## Guardrails (from technology-selection skill)
- **Temperature = 0** for factual Q&A
- **Model version pinning** (e.g., `gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18`)
- **Retry with backoff** via `RetryingChatClient`
- **Max output tokens** capped
- **Relevance threshold** (0.75 min similarity) to avoid injecting noise
- **Source attribution** — every answer cites which PDF pages it drew from
- **No hardcoded secrets** — API key via user-secrets / env vars
- **Streaming responses** for low time-to-first-token UX
- **Embedding caching** — never re-embed already-ingested content
- **Graceful degradation** — if no relevant chunks found, say "I don't have information on that"

## Notes
- The `Docs/` folder already contains `product-guide.pdf` — this will be the initial ingestion target
- Any new PDFs dropped into `Docs/` will be picked up on next app start or manual re-ingest
- Chat history is per-session, stored in Postgres for auditability
- No agentic loops needed — this is pure RAG (retrieve + generate), so `IChatClient` is the correct layer (no `Microsoft.Agents.AI` needed)

- MEAI's IChatClient is the right fit here since we're doing straightforward RAG without needing the Agent Framework

The architecture breaks down into four main pieces: a Blazor Server chat interface, a PDF ingestion pipeline that parses documents and stores embeddings in pgvector, a query pipeline that retrieves relevant context and generates responses, and the Postgres database with pgvector extension backing everything.

For the tech stack, I'll use Microsoft.Extensions.AI as the LLM abstraction layer with OpenAI as the provider, Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions for vector operations, Npgsql with the pgvector connector for database access, PdfPig for PDF parsing, and Entity Framework Core to manage chat history.

# Help Chatbot — Implementation Plan

## Problem
Build a web-based, LLM-driven chatbot using Blazor that answers customer questions by searching internal product PDF documentation. Use Postgres (with pgvector) for data storage and a local `Docs/` folder for PDF files.

## Approach — RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation)
This is a **RAG pipeline**: ingest PDFs → chunk text → generate embeddings → store in pgvector → on each user query, retrieve relevant chunks → inject into LLM prompt → return grounded answer with source attribution.

Per the .NET AI technology-selection guidance:
- **Microsoft.Extensions.AI** (`IChatClient` / `IEmbeddingGenerator`) — LLM abstraction layer (single prompt→response, no agentic loops needed)
- **OpenAI** — concrete LLM provider (gpt-4o-mini for chat, text-embedding-3-small for embeddings)
- **Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions** + **pgvector** — vector similarity search
- **UglyToad.PdfPig** — PDF text extraction (MIT licensed)
- **Npgsql / EF Core** — Postgres access for chat history
- **Blazor Server** (interactive SSR) — real-time chat UI with streaming responses

## Architecture

```
┌──────────────┐       ┌──────────────────┐       ┌────────────┐
│  Blazor UI   │──────▶│  ChatService     │──────▶│  OpenAI    │
│  (Chat page) │◀──────│  (RAG pipeline)  │◀──────│  LLM API   │
└──────────────┘       └────────┬─────────┘       └────────────┘
                                │
                    ┌───────────┼───────────┐
                    ▼                       ▼
            ┌──────────────┐       ┌──────────────┐
            │  pgvector    │       │  Postgres    │
            │  (embeddings)│       │  (chat hist) │
            └──────────────┘       └──────────────┘
                    ▲
                    │
            ┌──────────────┐       ┌──────────┐
            │ Ingestion    │◀──────│ Docs/    │
            │ Service      │       │ (PDFs)   │
            └──────────────┘       └──────────┘
```

## Todos

### 1. Project setup & packages
- Add NuGet packages: `Microsoft.Extensions.AI`, `OpenAI`, `Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions`, pgvector MEVD connector, `UglyToad.PdfPig`, `Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL`, Blazor component packages
- Configure `appsettings.json` with Postgres connection string and AI options (model IDs, temperature)
- Set up user-secrets for API key storage (never in source)

### 2. Data models
- `DocumentChunk` — record for vector store (Id, Text, Embedding, SourceFile, PageNumber)
- `ChatMessage` — EF entity for chat history (Id, SessionId, Role, Content, Timestamp)
- `ChatSession` — EF entity (Id, CreatedAt)

### 3. Postgres + pgvector setup
- EF Core `ChatDbContext` for chat history tables
- MEVD vector store registration for `DocumentChunk`
- Startup migration / ensure-created logic

### 4. PDF ingestion service
- `IngestionService` — scans `Docs/` folder for PDFs
- Extract text with PdfPig, split into semantic chunks (~500 tokens, paragraph-boundary aware)
- Generate embeddings via `IEmbeddingGenerator<string, Embedding<float>>`
- Upsert chunks into pgvector store
- Runs at startup (skip already-ingested files) and can be triggered manually

### 5. RAG chat service
- `ChatService.AskAsync(string question, string sessionId)`
- Embed the user query
- Search pgvector for top-5 relevant chunks (min similarity 0.75)
- Build system prompt with retrieved context + source attribution instructions
- Call `IChatClient` with streaming (`GetStreamingResponseAsync`)
- Persist Q&A to chat history in Postgres

### 6. Blazor UI
- `Components/Pages/Chat.razor` — chat interface with message bubbles, input box, send button
- Streaming token display via `StreamRendering`
- Chat history sidebar showing past sessions
- Source attribution displayed beneath answers (file name + page number)
- `Components/Layout/` — minimal layout with app title

### 7. Service registration (Program.cs)
- Register `IChatClient` via `AddChatClient` with OpenAI provider + retry middleware
- Register `IEmbeddingGenerator` for embeddings
- Register MEVD vector store with pgvector
- Register EF Core with Npgsql
- Register `IngestionService`, `ChatService`
- Set temperature=0, pin model versions, set max output tokens
- Add health checks for OpenAI and Postgres

### 8. Configuration & security
- `appsettings.json` — non-secret config (model IDs, chunk size, connection string template)
- User-secrets for `AI:ApiKey`
- `IOptions<AiOptions>` pattern for all AI config

## Guardrails (from technology-selection skill)
- **Temperature = 0** for factual Q&A
- **Model version pinning** (e.g., `gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18`)
- **Retry with backoff** via `RetryingChatClient`
- **Max output tokens** capped
- **Relevance threshold** (0.75 min similarity) to avoid injecting noise
- **Source attribution** — every answer cites which PDF pages it drew from
- **No hardcoded secrets** — API key via user-secrets / env vars
- **Streaming responses** for low time-to-first-token UX
- **Embedding caching** — never re-embed already-ingested content
- **Graceful degradation** — if no relevant chunks found, say "I don't have information on that"

## Notes
- The `Docs/` folder already contains `product-guide.pdf` — this will be the initial ingestion target
- Any new PDFs dropped into `Docs/` will be picked up on next app start or manual re-ingest
- Chat history is per-session, stored in Postgres for auditability
- No agentic loops needed — this is pure RAG (retrieve + generate), so `IChatClient` is the correct layer (no `Microsoft.Agents.AI` needed)

# Help Chatbot — Implementation Plan

## Problem
Build a web-based, LLM-driven chatbot using Blazor that answers customer questions by searching internal product PDF documentation. Use Postgres (with pgvector) for data storage and a local `Docs/` folder for PDF files.

## Approach — RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation)
This is a **RAG pipeline**: ingest PDFs → chunk text → generate embeddings → store in pgvector → on each user query, retrieve relevant chunks → inject into LLM prompt → return grounded answer with source attribution.

Per the .NET AI technology-selection guidance:
- **Microsoft.Extensions.AI** (`IChatClient` / `IEmbeddingGenerator`) — LLM abstraction layer (single prompt→response, no agentic loops needed)
- **OpenAI** — concrete LLM provider (gpt-4o-mini for chat, text-embedding-3-small for embeddings)
- **Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions** + **pgvector** — vector similarity search
- **UglyToad.PdfPig** — PDF text extraction (MIT licensed)
- **Npgsql / EF Core** — Postgres access for chat history
- **Blazor Server** (interactive SSR) — real-time chat UI with streaming responses

## Architecture

```
┌──────────────┐       ┌──────────────────┐       ┌────────────┐
│  Blazor UI   │──────▶│  ChatService     │──────▶│  OpenAI    │
│  (Chat page) │◀──────│  (RAG pipeline)  │◀──────│  LLM API   │
└──────────────┘       └────────┬─────────┘       └────────────┘
                                │
                    ┌───────────┼───────────┐
                    ▼                       ▼
            ┌──────────────┐       ┌──────────────┐
            │  pgvector    │       │  Postgres    │
            │  (embeddings)│       │  (chat hist) │
            └──────────────┘       └──────────────┘
                    ▲
                    │
            ┌──────────────┐       ┌──────────┐
            │ Ingestion    │◀──────│ Docs/    │
            │ Service      │       │ (PDFs)   │
            └──────────────┘       └──────────┘
```

## Todos

### 1. Project setup & packages
- Add NuGet packages: `Microsoft.Extensions.AI`, `OpenAI`, `Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions`, pgvector MEVD connector, `UglyToad.PdfPig`, `Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL`
- Configure `appsettings.json` with Postgres connection string and AI options (model IDs, temperature)
- Set up user-secrets for API key storage (never in source)

### 2. Data models
- `DocumentChunk` — record for vector store (Id, Text, Embedding, SourceFile, PageNumber)
- `ChatMessage` — EF entity for chat history (Id, SessionId, Role, Content, Timestamp)
- `ChatSession` — EF entity (Id, CreatedAt)

### 3. Postgres + pgvector setup
- EF Core `ChatDbContext` for chat history tables
- MEVD vector store registration for `DocumentChunk`
- Startup migration / ensure-created logic

### 4. PDF ingestion service
- `IngestionService` — scans `Docs/` folder for PDFs
- Extract text with PdfPig, split into semantic chunks (~500 tokens, paragraph-boundary aware)
- Generate embeddings via `IEmbeddingGenerator<string, Embedding<float>>`
- Upsert chunks into pgvector store
- Runs at startup (skip already-ingested files) and can be triggered manually

### 5. RAG chat service
- `ChatService.AskAsync(string question, string sessionId)`
- Embed the user query
- Search pgvector for top-5 relevant chunks (min similarity 0.75)
- Build system prompt with retrieved context + source attribution instructions
- Call `IChatClient` with streaming (`GetStreamingResponseAsync`)
- Persist Q&A to chat history in Postgres

### 6. Blazor UI
- `Components/Pages/Chat.razor` — chat interface with message bubbles, input box, send button
- Streaming token display via `StreamRendering`
- Chat history sidebar showing past sessions
- Source attribution displayed beneath answers (file name + page number)
- `Components/Layout/` — minimal layout with app title

### 7. Service registration (Program.cs)
- Register `IChatClient` via `AddChatClient` with OpenAI provider + retry middleware
- Register `IEmbeddingGenerator` for embeddings
- Register MEVD vector store with pgvector
- Register EF Core with Npgsql
- Register `IngestionService`, `ChatService`
- Set temperature=0, pin model versions, set max output tokens
- Add health checks for OpenAI and Postgres

### 8. Configuration & security
- `appsettings.json` — non-secret config (model IDs, chunk size, connection string template)
- User-secrets for `AI:ApiKey`
- `IOptions<AiOptions>` pattern for all AI config

## Guardrails (from technology-selection guidance)
- **Temperature = 0** for factual Q&A
- **Model version pinning** (e.g., `gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18`)
- **Retry with backoff** via `RetryingChatClient`
- **Max output tokens** capped
- **Relevance threshold** (0.75 min similarity) to avoid injecting noise
- **Source attribution** — every answer cites which PDF pages it drew from
- **No hardcoded secrets** — API key via user-secrets / env vars
- **Streaming responses** for low time-to-first-token UX
- **Embedding caching** — never re-embed already-ingested content
- **Graceful degradation** — if no relevant chunks found, say "I don't have information on that"

## Notes
- The `Docs/` folder already contains `product-guide.pdf` — this will be the initial ingestion target
- Any new PDFs dropped into `Docs/` will be picked up on next app start or manual re-ingest
- Chat history is per-session, stored in Postgres for auditability
- No agentic loops needed — this is pure RAG (retrieve + generate), so `IChatClient` is the correct layer (no `Microsoft.Agents.AI` required)

```
            │ Service      │       │ (PDFs)   │
            └──────────────┘       └──────────┘
```

## Todos

### 1. Project setup & packages
- Add NuGet packages: `Microsoft.Extensions.AI`, `OpenAI`, `Microsoft.Extensions.VectorData.Abstractions`, pgvector MEVD connector, `UglyToad.PdfPig`, `Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL`
- Configure `appsettings.json` with Postgres connection string and AI options (model IDs, temperature)
- Set up user-secrets for API key storage (never in source)

### 2. Data models
- `DocumentChunk` — record for vector store (Id, Text, Embedding, SourceFile, PageNumber)
- `ChatMessage` — EF entity for chat history (Id, SessionId, Role, Content, Timestamp)
- `ChatSession` — EF entity (Id, CreatedAt)

### 3. Postgres + pgvector setup
- EF Core `ChatDbContext` for chat history tables
- MEVD vector store registration for `DocumentChunk`
- Startup migration / ensure-created logic

### 4. PDF ingestion service
- `IngestionService` — scans `Docs/` folder for PDFs
- Extract text with PdfPig, split into semantic chunks (~500 tokens, paragraph-boundary aware)
- Generate embeddings via `IEmbeddingGenerator<string, Embedding<float>>`
- Upsert chunks into pgvector store
- Runs at startup (skip already-ingested files) and can be triggered manually

### 5. RAG chat service
- `ChatService.AskAsync(string question, string sessionId)`
- Embed the user query
- Search pgvector for top-5 relevant chunks (min similarity 0.75)
- Build system prompt with retrieved context + source attribution instructions
- Call `IChatClient` with streaming (`GetStreamingResponseAsync`)
- Persist Q&A to chat history in Postgres

### 6. Blazor UI
- `Components/Pages/Chat.razor` — chat interface with message bubbles, input box, send button
- Streaming token display via `StreamRendering`
- Chat history sidebar showing past sessions
- Source attribution displayed beneath answers (file name + page number)
- `Components/Layout/` — minimal layout with app title

### 7. Service registration (Program.cs)
- Register `IChatClient` via `AddChatClient` with OpenAI provider + retry middleware
- Register `IEmbeddingGenerator` for embeddings
- Register MEVD vector store with pgvector
- Register EF Core with Npgsql
- Register `IngestionService`, `ChatService`
- Set temperature=0, pin model versions, set max output tokens
- Add health checks for OpenAI and Postgres

### 8. Configuration & security
- `appsettings.json` — non-secret config (model IDs, chunk size, connection string template)
- User-secrets for `AI:ApiKey`
- `IOptions<AiOptions>` pattern for all AI config

## Guardrails
- **Temperature = 0** for factual Q&A
- **Model version pinning** (e.g., `gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18`)
- **Retry with backoff** via `RetryingChatClient`
- **Max output tokens** capped
- **Relevance threshold** (0.75 min similarity) to avoid injecting noise
- **Source attribution** — every answer cites which PDF pages it drew from
- **No hardcoded secrets** — API key via user-secrets / env vars
- **Streaming responses** for low time-to-first-token UX
- **Embedding caching** — never re-embed already-ingested content
- **Graceful degradation** — if no relevant chunks found, say "I don't have information on that"

## Notes
- The `Docs/` folder already contains `product-guide.pdf` — this will be the initial ingestion target
- Any new PDFs dropped into `Docs/` will be picked up on next app start or manual re-ingest
- Chat history is per-session, stored in Postgres for auditability
- No agentic loops needed — this is pure RAG (retrieve + generate), so `IChatClient` is the correct layer (no `Microsoft.Agents.AI` required)
